content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · · Administration · · · Executive/Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5662          322 5660              322 5901            322 6441         322 6444

September 14, 1979

Jim Darrow
City Attorney
1275 N. Main Street
El Centro, CA 92243

Dear Mr. Darrow:

     The purpose of this letter is to confirm in writing the advice I gave you during our telephone conversation of September 12, 1979. The facts as presented to me were as follows.

     Councilman Dale Garris has a 25% interest in a closely held corporation known as Quad G. The corporation has a limited partnership interest worth $12,500 in a joint venture with Robert Childers, a developer. The Quad G/Childers partnership owns 117 acres in the El Centro area. Twenty-five of the 117 acres are currently being developed for light commercial and residential use in accordance with a tentative map approved by the city council. The remaining undeveloped acreage has been zoned by both the city and county for low-density residential development.

     Mr. Childers has formed other partnerships, in which Quad G does not participate, for the purposes of developing land parcels in the southern part of El Centro which is now separated from the downtown area by Interstate 8. It is contemplated that a hotel, golf course, condominium and an athletic complex may be built in the area. Neither Councilman Garris nor Quad G have any direct or indirect interests in the limited partnerships formed by Mr. Childers for these development activities. Mr. Childers, as representative of these limited partnerships, and not the Quad G/Childers venture, is negotiating with the city a cost sharing plan to build a freeway overpass in order to provide access between the down-town area and the proposed area of development.

Mr. Jim Darrow
September 14, 1979
Page Two


     You have asked whether Councilman Garris is disqualified
from participating in council decisions regarding the proposed
freeway overpass.  I advised you that Government Code Section
87100 requires public officials to disqualify themselves from
making or participating in decisions in which they have finan-
cial interests.

     Government Code Section 87103 provides:

     An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from
its effect on the public generally, on:

     (a)  Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

. . .

     (c)  Any source of income, other than loans by
a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
promised to the public official within twelve
months prior to the time when the decision is
made;

. . .

     As a result of a 25% interest in Quad G, and Quad G's
$12,500 investment in the Quad G/Childers venture, Councilman
Garris has an investment over $1,000 in the Quad G/Childers
venture for the purposes of Government Code Sections 87100 and
87103.  Thus, Councilman Garris must refrain from participating
in the freeway overpass negotiations if it is foreseeable that
the building of the overpass or the failure to build it will
materially affect Quad G or the partnership with Childers.  In
addition, if it is foreseeable that the council decision regarding the overpass might affect Mr. Childers' other financial
interests to such a degree that the Quad G/Childers venture
might be financially affected, then Councilman Garris is
obligated to disqualify himself.

     The Act also requires public officials to disqualify
themselves from making or participating in decisions which
foreseeably will have a material financial effect on their
sources of income.  Thus, if Mr. Childers is a source of income

Mr. Jim Darrow
September 14, 1979
Page Three


to Councilman Garris, Councilman Garris should disqualify himself
from participating in the overpass negotiations. However, the
existence of a partnership relationship does not make partners
sources of income to each other.

I hope that my advice is helpful. If I can be of further
assistance, please contact me.

Sincerely,

Dwight Dickerson
Legal Counsel
Conflicts of Interest Division

DED:mfa

sections.question_synthetic: Is Councilman Dale Garris disqualified from participating in city council decisions regarding a proposed freeway overpass under Government Code Section 87100, given his 25% interest in Quad G corporation, which has a partnership interest in a venture with developer Robert Childers who is separately negotiating the overpass deal?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Councilman Garris must disqualify himself from freeway overpass negotiations if it is foreseeable that the overpass decision will materially affect the Quad G/Childers venture or if Mr. Childers is a source of income to him (though partnership relationships alone do not create source-of-income conflicts). His 25% interest in Quad G, which has over $1,000 invested in the partnership, triggers disqualification requirements under Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a written confirmation of telephonic advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections marked with headers; question and conclusion extracted from body text. Letter format with clear factual recitation followed by legal analysis. Addresses conflict of interest disqualification requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is Councilman Dale Garris disqualified from participating in city council decisions regarding a proposed freeway overpass under Government Code Section 87100, given his 25% interest in Quad G corporation, which has a partnership interest in a venture with developer Robert Childers who is separately negotiating the overpass deal?

CONCLUSION: Councilman Garris must disqualify himself from freeway overpass negotiations if it is foreseeable that the overpass decision will materially affect the Quad G/Childers venture or if Mr. Childers is a source of income to him (though partnership relationships alone do not create source-of-income conflicts). His 25% interest in Quad G, which has over $1,000 invested in the partnership, triggers disqualification requirements under Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103.

embedding.summary: FPPC Legal Counsel Dwight Dickerson advises City Attorney Jim Darrow that Councilman Dale Garris must disqualify himself from decisions regarding a proposed freeway overpass if the decision will foreseeably have a material financial effect on Quad G or the Quad G/Childers venture in which he has a financial interest exceeding $1,000.
